command.name: pulumi
command.description: manage infrastructure as code resources with Pulumi

options.description: show help
colorize output
run pulumi as if it had been started in another directory
disable integrity checking of checkpoint files
enable emojis in the output
flow log settings to child processes
log to stderr instead of files
disable interactive mode for all commands
emit CPU and memory profiles and an execution trace
emit tracing to the specified endpoint
enable verbose logging

subcommands.name: about
subcommands.description: print information about the Pulumi environment

options.description: emit output as json
include transitive dependencies

subcommands.name: cancel
subcommands.description: cancel a stack’s currently running update, if any

options.description: skip confirmation prompts
stack name to operate on

subcommands.name: config
subcommands.description: manage configuration

options.description: emit output as json
stack name to operate on
use configuration values from the specified file
show secret values when listing config instead of displaying blinded values

subcommands.name: cp
subcommands.description: copy config to another stack

options.description: the name of the new stack to copy the config to

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: get a single configuration value

options.description: the key contains a path to a property in a map or list to get

subcommands.name: refresh
subcommands.description: update the local configuration based on the most recent deployment of the stack

options.description: overwrite configuration file, if it exists, without creating a backup

subcommands.name: rm
subcommands.description: remove configuration value

options.description: the key contains a path to a property in a map or list to remove

subcommands.name: rm-all
subcommands.description: remove multiple configuration values

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: set configuration value

options.description: save the value as plaintext (unencrypted)
encrypt the value instead of storing it in plaintext

subcommands.name: set-all
subcommands.description: set multiple configuration values

options.description: marks a value as plaintext (unencrypted)
marks a value as secret to be encrypted

subcommands.name: console
subcommands.description: opens the current stack in the Pulumi Console

options.description: stack name to operate on

subcommands.name: destroy
subcommands.description: destroy an existing stack and its resources

options.description: stack name to operate on
number of resource operations to run in parallel
message to associate with the operation
display operation as a rich diff
print detailed debugging output during resource operations
use configuration values from the specified file
suppress display of the state permalink
resource URN to target
emit output as json
refresh resource state before the update
show configuration keys and variables
show detailed resource replacement creates and deletes
show unchanged resources
suppress display of stack outputs
allow destroying discovered dependent targets
skip confirmation prompts
do not perform a preview before the operation
do not destroy protected resources. Destroy all other resources

subcommands.name: import
subcommands.description: import resources into an existing stack

options.description: use configuration values from the specified file
print detailed debugging output during resource operations
display operation as a rich diff
message to associate with the operation
number of resource operations to run in parallel
stack name to operate on
skip confirmation prompts
do not perform a preview before the operation
suppress display of the state permalink
the path to a JSON-encoded file containing a list of resources to import
the path to the file that will contain the generated resource declarations
the name and URN of the parent resource in the format name=urn, where name is the variable name of the parent resource
allow resources to be imported with protection from deletion enabled (default true)
the name and URN of the provider to use for the import in the format name=urn, where name is the variable name for the provider resourceƒ
suppress display of stack outputs (in case they contain sensitive values)

subcommands.name: login
subcommands.description: log in to the Pulumi service

options.description: use Pulumi in local-only mode
a cloud URL to log in to
a default org to associate with the login. Please note, currently, only the managed and self-hosted backends support organizations

subcommands.name: logout
subcommands.description: log out of the Pulumi service

options.description: use Pulumi in local-only mode
logout of all backends
a cloud URL to log out of (defaults to current cloud)

subcommands.name: logs
subcommands.description: show aggregated resource logs for a stack

options.description: use configuration values from the specified file
stack name to operate on
emit output as json
follow the log stream in real time (like tail -f)
only return logs for the requested resource ('name', 'type::name' or full URN). Defaults to returning all logs
only return logs newer than a relative duration ('5s', '2m', '3h') or absolute timestamp. Defaults to returning the last 1 hour of logs. (default "1h")

subcommands.name: new

options.description: stack name to operate on
skip confirmation prompts
provider used to encrypt and decrypt secrets
config to save
config keys contain a path to a property in a map or list to set
the project description; if not specified, a prompt will request it
the location to place the generated project; if not specified, the current directory is used
forces content to be generated even if it would change existing files
generate the project only; do not create a stack, save config, or install dependencies
the project name; if not specified, a prompt will request it
use locally cached templates without making any network requests
